Title: A chemical genetic strategy identify the PHOSTIN, a synthetic molecule that triggers phosphate starvation responses in Arabidopsis thaliana

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: WS (reporter line); Col-0/Coler105 for most assays
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PHT1 family high-affinity inorganic phosphate transporter
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential macronutrient for plant growth and phosphate homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PSN/PSN11 strongly induces PHT1;4 expression, consistent with enhanced root Pi uptake and increased root Pi accumulation under Pi-sufficient conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHT1;4::GUS was the primary screening marker used to identify PHOSTIN activity. 2. PSN, PSN2, PSN8 and PSN11 induced PHT1;4 expression in +Pi conditions. 3. Increased 33Pi uptake in PSN-treated roots is consistent with induced high-affinity Pi transporter activity.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically and strongly induced by low Pi in root epidermis and root tip
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in roots by PSN/PSN11 under Pi-replete conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter::GUS reporter assay; split-root assay; 33Pi uptake assay; metabolite/Pi content measurements
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: +Pi versus -Pi; PSN, PSN2, PSN8, PSN11 treatment; split-root conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker and engineering target to enhance phosphate uptake efficiency, but activation without coordinated shoot translocation may uncouple uptake from whole-plant Pi balance
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PSN-induced activation coincides with primary root growth inhibition and altered Pi partitioning; increased root Pi does not increase shoot Pi
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene is used mainly as a core experimental marker of phosphate-starvation signaling and transporter-linked Pi uptake rather than as the discovered causal target of PSN.

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Columbia-derived mutant lines pho1-2 and PHO1-B1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PHO1 is not required for PSN effects on Pi redistribution or induction of phosphate-starvation responses
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHO1-deficient lines overaccumulate Pi in roots under normal conditions. 2. PSN still increased Pi content in both roots and shoots of PHO1-deficient lines. 3. Therefore PHO1 activity is dispensable for PSN-triggered Pi-starvation-like responses.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: PHO1-H1 transcript is induced by PSN11 in WT roots but not in phr1;phl1 mutant
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis (pho1-2, PHO1-B1); Pi content measurements; qRT-PCR for PHO1-H1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important comparator for dissecting Pi translocation versus signaling components; not a sufficient target for mimicking PSN chemistry
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PHO1 impairment alters root/shoot Pi balance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core tested gene because the Results specifically show PSN effects are independent of PHO1. The induced transcript measured under PSN11 was PHO1-H1, not PHO1 itself.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: systemic control of phosphate allocation and homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PHO2 is not required for PSN-mediated changes in Pi balance; PSN partially restores root Pi in pho2 mutant
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pho2 mutants normally transfer more Pi to shoots and have reduced root Pi. 2. PSN treatment substantially reverted the reduced root Pi content of pho2. 3. Thus PSN acts independently of the PHO2 systemic pathway for this phenotype.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparator for separating known systemic Pi-homeostasis pathways from chemically triggered early signaling steps
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of PHO2 causes shoot Pi overaccumulation and altered Pi partitioning
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results explicitly test whether PSN requires PHO2 and conclude it does not.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: WT Columbia and phr1-3;phl1-2 double mutant
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pi starvation acclimation and phosphate homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PSN11-dependent transcriptional activation of low-Pi marker genes requires PHR1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PSN11 induced ACP5, IPS1, PHO1-H1 and PLDzeta2 in WT roots. 2. This induction was lost in the phr1-3;phl1-2 double mutant. 3. Therefore PHR1 is necessary for PSN11-triggered low-Pi transcriptional responses and PSN11 likely acts upstream of PHR1/PHL1.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phosphate starvation response and phosphate homeostasis signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master regulator necessary for PSN11-induced low-Pi gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ACP5; IPS1; PHO1-H1; PLDzeta2; PHT1;4-related low-Pi program
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates low-Pi responsive genes required for local and systemic phosphate-starvation responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double-mutant analysis (phr1-3;phl1-2); qRT-PCR of low-Pi marker genes after PSN11 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function assayed in roots after 4 d PSN11 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central lever for phosphate-use-efficiency engineering, but likely pleiotropic due to broad control of Pi-starvation responses
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad activation of starvation responses can inhibit primary root growth and alter metabolism/allocation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phosphate starvation status; PSN11 acts upstream or at the level of its signaling control
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper treats PHR1 together with its paralog PHL1 as the only known regulators of both systemic and some local Pi responses relevant here.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: WT Columbia and phr1-3;phl1-2 double mutant
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pi starvation acclimation and phosphate homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PHL1, together with PHR1, is required for PSN11-induced low-Pi transcriptional responses
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of PHR1 plus PHL1 abolished PSN11 induction of several low-Pi marker genes. 2. The double mutant demonstrates that PHL1 contributes essentially with PHR1 to the PSN11-responsive signaling module. 3. PSN11 therefore likely targets a signaling step upstream of the PHR1/PHL1 regulatory node.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phosphate starvation response and phosphate homeostasis signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: necessary co-regulator with PHR1 for PSN11-triggered low-Pi gene induction
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: supports activation of low-Pi responsive genes in conjunction with PHR1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function assayed in roots after PSN11 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential node for tuning phosphate-starvation signaling, but likely requires balanced modulation with PHR1 due to network-wide effects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation may broadly affect adaptive starvation responses
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phosphate starvation status; PSN11-responsive upstream signaling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific accession number for PHL1 was not given in the paper text provided.
